reason:
The node mentions three possible frameworks for the real case -- 'Jordan algebras or real order unit spaces rather than complex *-algebras' -- but does not mention the quaternionic case, which completes the Hurwitz classification. 

By Wedderburn theory and the Artin-Wedderburn theorem extended to real division algebras, finite-dimensional real *-algebras (real C*-algebras) have simple factors isomorphic to M_n(R), M_n(C), or M_n(H) (quaternion matrices). For the quaternionic case, the self-adjoint (self-dual) part of M_r(H) has dimension r(2r-1), giving yet another face dimension formula. This is the 'third case' in the Barvinok-Pataki classification that appears in some references (e.g., Barvinok's original paper discusses real, complex, and quaternionic cases).

While omitting the quaternionic case is not an error (and may be intentional for simplicity), a node about 'real vs complex subtlety' in the context of face dimensions would be more complete if it at least acknowledged the quaternionic case exists, especially since the paper's framework via operator algebras is well-suited to treat all three cases uniformly.
